content.full_text:
December 9, 1991

California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Sirs:

I am requesting advice as to a potential conflict of interest on the part of two members of the Modesto City Council. The City is in the process of negotiating the acquisition of Del Este, a private water company, to add to the city water system. Future financing of needed improvements to water service in the Del Este service area may require the Council to impose a surcharge on people in that area. The estimated city population which would be affected by a surcharge on the Del Este area is 45,736. An estimated 126,000 city residents will receive water service at different rates.

One councilmember resides in the Del Este service area and receives Del Este water service. Another councilmember owns two rental units which are in the Del Este service area. He is responsible for paying for Del Este service for the tenants. Do these councilmembers have a conflict of interest in voting on (1) acquisition of the private water service company; and (2) a rate surcharge for water users in the Del Este service area? The amount of the surcharge is not yet known so the actual change in water service costs to former Del Este customers due to the acquisition is not definite. However, assuming that future rate increases result in rate increases of over $250 per year per user, would the effect on the public official of a rate surcharge be distinguishable from the effect of the decision on the public generally? The percentage of people in the Del Este area affected by the rate increase are roughly 36% of the total population of the city.

I would appreciate your prompt advice on this matter.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
LAURY L. DOWD
Senior Deputy City Attorney

LLD/sw

The image is completely blank, with no visible text, numbers, or any other content. It appears to be an empty page or a page with no markings.
